name: chr1_olfactory_cluster
description: Olfactory receptor (OR) gene cluster on chr1, the largest single OR cluster in the human genome. Spans the chr1q23-q25 (proximal q-arm) region with ~120 OR genes plus ~80 OR pseudogenes, organised as tandem and inverted duplications. Member genes are 7-TM G-protein-coupled receptors of ~310 amino acids; intra-cluster identity is typically 60-95% at the nucleotide level, with hotspots of >98% identity in close-tandem duplicates that force PSV-aware mapping.
expected_copy_count: ~120 functional OR genes + ~80 pseudogenes per haplotype; segregating CNVs of single subfamily blocks observed in population data
